diagnosis: Hook infrastructure edit during analysis session, not skill friction

suggested_fix: Operators ['&&', '||'] found. Split into separate Bash tool calls per builder/bash-commands.md — one command per block.
root_cause: Debug command used && and || operators to check flag existence. This violates bash-commands.md rule forbidding chained operators.
proposed_fix: Use single-command approach: test -f .claude/hooks/health-agent/pending-ai-review.flag for exit code, or split into separate Bash calls.

suggested_fix: Split into separate Bash tool calls; pipe is disallowed by builder/bash-commands.md
root_cause: Debug analysis piped grep output to sort and uniq-c. Pipes violate one-command-per-block rule.
proposed_fix: Split: (1) grep -o to file, (2) Read the file, (3) process with Python if sorting/deduplication needed.

suggested_fix: Split into separate Bash tool calls; pipe is disallowed by builder/bash-commands.md
root_cause: Debug session piped ls output to head for limiting results. Violates one-command-per-block rule.
proposed_fix: Use single command or split: ls -t with awk/sed for filtering, or Read full listing and process in Python.

suggested_fix: Operators ['&&', '||'] found. Split into separate Bash tool calls per builder/bash-commands.md — one command per block.
root_cause: Another debug command using && and || to conditionally echo. Violates one-command-per-block rule.
proposed_fix: Replace with test command or Bash invocation with single logic: test -f path && return 0 or 1 as needed.

root_cause: Health infrastructure may invoke Skill tool generically during analysis. This is introspection, not user-facing friction. A bare Skill() invocation without arguments should not require whitelisting.
proposed_fix: This is a detector false positive. Update whitelist_gap detector to exclude bare Skill() invocations (no arguments), or filter Skill introspection from permission events during health analysis.
